Aggregated execute batches: completed 6/6.
Batch 1: Implemented batch 1 tasks T1, T2, and T4, and checkpointed progress at `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_1.json`.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. Targeted vitest runs exposed existing unrelated failures in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`; those suites were still run in full with the batch changes applied.
Batch 2: Implemented T3 in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`: stale shot-group IDs are now consumed as typed props, `hasStaleVideo` is derived into `PositionedShotGroup`, the shot-group menu payload includes stale state, and an amber `RefreshCw` badge opens the group context menu when a newer final video exists. Checkpointed batch progress to `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_2.json`.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. The full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module still has the same pre-existing pinned-shot expectation failures already noted in the prior batch (`data-shot-group-kind` assertions and `Unpin` label expectations).
Batch 3: Implemented T5: `TimelineEditor` now destructures `updateToLatestVideo`, wraps it in a local handler, passes `staleShotGroupIds` as a typed prop, and forwards the new update callback into `TimelineCanvas`, which now passes it through to `ShotGroupContextMenu`. Checkpointed batch progress to `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_3.json`.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. The full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module still shows the same pre-existing pinned-shot expectation failures from earlier batches (`data-shot-group-kind` assertions and stale `Unpin` label expectations).
Batch 4: Completed T6. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed cleanly against the current changeset, so no additional code edits were required. Checkpointed batch progress to `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Executed T7 verification. Ran the full vitest scope for `src/tools/video-editor` and `src/tools/travel-between-images`, ran the required throwaway TypeScript stale-shot-group probe with `node --import tsx`, deleted the probe, re-ran typecheck, and checkpointed results to `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_5.json`.
Batch 6: Executed T8 final verification. Ran a throwaway stale-video reproduction script with `node --import tsx`, confirmed it passed, deleted the script, reran the full existing vitest scope for `src/tools/video-editor` and `src/tools/travel-between-images`, and checkpointed the batch result to `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_6.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added and verified the stale-shot-group memo in TimelineEditor. It compares `resolvedConfig.registry[videoAssetKey]?.generationId` against `finalVideoMap.get(shotId)?.id`, skips missing registry/final-video cases, emits `${shotId}:${trackId}` keys, and passes the derived set toward TimelineCanvas for later typed wiring.

Implemented `updateToLatestVideo` in `useSwitchToFinalVideo`. It finds the video-mode pinned group, registers a fresh final-video asset, patches only the existing clip's `meta.asset`, updates `videoAssetKey` on the pinned group, and leaves rows, clip timing, duration span, and `imageClipSnapshot` unchanged.

Extended `ShotGroupContextMenu` with `hasStaleVideo` state and an optional `onUpdateToLatestVideo` callback, then inserted the `Update to Latest Video` action between the existing image actions and default actions. Rendering is gated to `menu.mode === 'video' && menu.hasStaleVideo`.

Verified TimelineCanvas now accepts `staleShotGroupIds`, derives `hasStaleVideo` per positioned shot group via the `${shotId}:${rowId}` key, threads that state into `ShotGroupMenuState`, and renders an amber `RefreshCw` badge that opens the shot-group context menu while remaining suppressed when `hasActiveTask` is true.

Verified `TimelineEditor` now destructures `updateToLatestVideo`, wraps it in a local callback that mirrors the existing final-video switch flow, and passes both that callback and the typed `staleShotGroupIds` prop into `TimelineCanvas`. Verified `TimelineCanvas` forwards `onShotGroupUpdateToLatestVideo` directly into `ShotGroupContextMenu`, preserving the `{ shotId, rowId }` payload through the full component chain.

Verified the repository compiles cleanly with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`. No follow-up edits were needed, and the newly added timeline props/callbacks remain optional where appropriate so unchanged call sites continue to typecheck.

Verified the current changes against the full `src/tools/video-editor` and `src/tools/travel-between-images` vitest scope, not individual tests. The run finished with 125 passing files and 3 failing files (`useClipEditing.test.ts`, `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `BatchModeContent.test.tsx`), then the required throwaway TypeScript probe confirmed the stale-shot-group type path and was deleted, followed by a clean `tsc` rerun.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor src/tools/travel-between-images

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the stale-video behavior with a throwaway reproduction script, then ran the full existing vitest scope for `src/tools/video-editor` and `src/tools/travel-between-images` as the final regression pass. The run still ends with 7 failing tests across `useClipEditing.test.ts`, `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and `BatchModeContent.test.tsx`, with no broader regression spread beyond those already-failing files.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor src/tools/travel-between-images

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The `useMemo` exits early when there are no pinned groups or no resolved registry, and each group is skipped if the registry entry or `finalVideoMap` entry is missing, so undefined registry slots and missing final videos do not produce stale IDs.

Yes. `updateToLatestVideo` is an in-place asset swap: it keeps `current.rows` unchanged and only updates the existing clip's `asset` plus the pinned group's `videoAssetKey`, so position, duration span, and `imageClipSnapshot` are preserved.
Yes. The new menu item only renders for video-mode groups with `hasStaleVideo=true`, and it is placed after `imageActions` and before `defaultActions`.

Yes. The stale badge uses the same absolute overlay placement, `zIndex: 9`, and `pointerEvents: 'auto'` pattern as the final-video badge, and it is hidden when `hasActiveTask` is true so it does not compete with the active-task indicator.

Yes. The new update callback follows the same `TimelineEditor -> TimelineCanvas -> ShotGroupContextMenu` threading pattern already used for `onSwitchToFinalVideo` and `onSwitchToImages`, and `TimelineCanvas` now forwards the callback without reshaping or dropping its `{ shotId, rowId }` payload.
Yes. TypeScript compiled with no new errors, and the newly introduced timeline props and callbacks are still optional on component interfaces so existing call sites remain valid.

No. The full video-editor verification sweep is not fully green: 7 tests still fail across `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/sections/generation/BatchModeContent.test.tsx`. The full suite was rerun with the current stale-video changes applied, so the image-mode sync path was checked under the current repository baseline even though existing failures remain.

No. The final verification tests did not pass cleanly: the suite still ends with the same 7 failing tests across `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/sections/generation/BatchModeContent.test.tsx`. The stale-video reproduction script passed, and no new failing files were introduced outside that existing baseline.
